title: Ship SD-JWT session tokens for off-chain proof reuse

artifacts.description: Mints SD-JWT session tokens from a HandshakeResult; binds nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, agentId, iat, exp (configurable TTL 60-3600s), and selective-disclosure salt per claim.
Verifies SD-JWT token signature, expiry, and presence of requiredClaims; returns decoded payload or throws typed errors.
SessionTokenOptions (ttl, issuer, audience, selectiveDisclosureFields), SessionTokenPayload, and SessionVerifyOptions (requiredClaims, clockSkew) interfaces.
Barrel export for SessionTokenIssuer, SessionTokenVerifier, and types.
Re-export session module from main SDK entry point.
Unit tests: mint returns compact JWT, TTL enforced, required claims present, nullifierHash and scopeCommitment bound correctly.
Unit tests: valid token passes, expired token throws SessionTokenExpiredError, missing requiredClaims throws SessionTokenClaimMissingError, tampered signature throws SessionTokenInvalidError.
End-to-end: verifyHandshake() → SessionTokenIssuer.mint() → SessionTokenVerifier.verify() round-trip with real handshake fixture.
Protocol spec: SD-JWT claim set definition, selective disclosure fields, TTL policy, replay prevention via nullifierHash binding, security considerations (token theft, clock skew).
Add 'Session Tokens' section showing 5-line mint+verify usage pattern after verifyHandshake().

steps: Add jose (or @sd-jwt/core) as a dependency in sdk/package.json; choose a library that supports selective disclosure natively to avoid rolling crypto.
Define SessionTokenPayload type: { nullifierHash: string, scopeCommitment: string, agentId: string, humanMerkleRoot: string, iat: number, exp: number } plus _sd array for selective disclosure.

Define SessionTokenOptions: { ttl: number (default 300, min 60, max 3600), issuer: string, audience?: string, signingKey: CryptoKey | string, selectiveDisclosureFields?: Array<keyof SessionTokenPayload> }.

Implement SessionTokenIssuer.mint(handshakeResult: HandshakeResult, opts: SessionTokenOptions): Promise<string> — extract claims from handshakeResult, apply SD-JWT encoding for selective fields, sign with HS256 or ES256 depending on key type, return compact token string.

Implement SessionTokenVerifier.verify(token: string, opts: SessionVerifyOptions): Promise<SessionTokenPayload> — decode header, verify signature, check exp vs Date.now() with configurable clockSkew (default 30s), assert all requiredClaims are disclosed, return decoded payload.
Define typed error classes: SessionTokenExpiredError, SessionTokenInvalidError, SessionTokenClaimMissingError — all extend BolyraError base so existing catch blocks work.
Write unit tests for issuer: happy path, TTL boundary (59s rejected at config time), selective disclosure omits undisclosed fields from decoded payload.
Write unit tests for verifier: tampered payload fails, expired token throws correct error, missing required claim throws correct error, clock skew tolerance respected.
Write integration test using real handshake fixture (mock prover output) to confirm full mint→verify round-trip without re-proving.
Export SessionTokenIssuer and SessionTokenVerifier from sdk/src/index.ts; bump SDK version to v0.3.0 in package.json.

Update spec/session-token-offchain.md with claim set table, replay-prevention rationale (nullifierHash is single-use at proof time; token is short-lived to bound window), and recommended TTL guidance per threat level.
Add 'Session Tokens' section to sdk/QUICKSTART.md with minimal 5-line example and note that tokens replace per-call ZK proving.
dependencies: HandshakeResult type from sdk/src/core/types.ts (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, agentId, humanMerkleRoot)
Existing verifyHandshake() function as the upstream entry point
Node 18+ SubtleCrypto for key generation in tests
